title: PHP File Handling
content:
### PHP File Handling

PHP provides a rich set of functions for working with files on the server.

### Opening a File

```php
<?php
$file = fopen("test.txt", "r") or die("Unable to open file!");
?>
```

### File Modes

| Mode | Name | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `r` | Read | Read from start |
| `r+` | Read/Write | Read and write from start |
| `w` | Write | Write from start (truncates) |
| `w+` | Read/Write | Read and write (truncates) |
| `a` | Append | Write to end |
| `a+` | Read/Append | Read and append |
| `x` | Create | Create for writing (fails if exists) |
| `x+` | Create | Create for read/write |

### Basic File Functions

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `fopen()` | Open a file |
| `fclose()` | Close a file |
| `fgets()` | Read a line |
| `fgetc()` | Read a character |
| `fwrite()` / `fputs()` | Write to a file |
| `feof()` | Check end of file |
| `filesize()` | Get file size |
| `file_exists()` | Check if file exists |

> **Key Point:** Always close files with `fclose()` when done to free system resources and prevent data corruption.
codeExample:
"; $file = fopen($filename, "r"); if ($file) { echo "File opened successfully
"; while (!feof($file)) { echo fgets($file) . "
"; } fclose($file); } } else { echo "File does not exist"; } ?>
